name: Red Pepper Relish
recipeIngredient: 1 Cup Red Bell Pepper, cut into 1/2-inch pieces
4 Serrano Or Jalapeno Chiles, finely minced
1 3/4 Cups Sugar, or more to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix red pepper, onion, garlic, chiles, and salt in a large bowl.
2. Let stand for 3 hours.
3. Drain thoroughly (do not rinse).
4. Combine remaining ingredients in a large saucepan and bring to boil over medium-high heat.
5. Boil for 10 minutes.
6. Reduce heat to medium, stir in pepper mixture, and cook until peppers are translucent but still slightly crunchy, about 30 to 45 minutes.
7. Pour relish into 1 clean, hot pint jar, leaving 1/2-inch from the top.
8. Use a plastic knife or spatula to release any air bubbles between the relish and jar.
9. Clean the rim and threads of the jar with a damp cloth.
10. Seal with a new, scalded, very hot lid.
11. Repeat with remaining jars.
12. Transfer jars to gently simmering (180 degrees to 190 degrees) water bath and process for 15 minutes.
13. Let jars cool on a rack.
14. Test for seal.
15. Store in a cool, dry place until ready to use.
Amount of sugar can be adjusted to taste: Use 2 1/4 cups for sweet relish, 1 3/4 cups for tangy. For a tarter version, use only 1 cup.
